channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Resident Evil Requiem Topped February 2026 Sales Charts
item.content:encoded:
Resident Evil Requiem was the best-selling game of February 2026, followed by several 2025 titles.

New video game sales data has been revealed, and we have been informed that Resident Evil Requiem has debuted as the best-selling game of 2026, outperforming the likes of Resident Evil Village. The latest chart shows it was the clear sales winner in February 2026.

Resident Evil Requiem was February 2026’s Best-Selling Game

Capcom has hit it out of the park with Resident Evil Requiem, with the title topping sales charts for all platforms. Mat Piscatella of Circana reveals that the game was number 1 in the United States, followed by NBA 2K26, Call of Duty: Black Ops 7, Minecraft, and Helldivers 2. The title ranked first on Xbox, PlayStation, and aggregated PC storefronts. The only platform that it didn’t top was Nintendo, which saw the release of Mario Tennis Fever, taking the cake.

The latest sales numbers for Resident Evil Requiem are unknown, but as of this week, the title has already sold over 6 million copies, making it the fastest-selling game in the franchise. It also outperformed Resident Evil Village, with Requiem’s launch week sales 60% higher and unit sales 40% higher than the previous game. There were several new video games among the month’s best sellers, including Mario Tennis Fever, God of War: Sons of Sparta, My Hero Academia: All’s Justice, and Resident Evil Generation Pack.

In other news, Resident Evil Requiem director has stated that Leon Kennedy’s spouse will be revealed someday. Additionally, Resident Evil Requiem’s director has confirmed that a story expansion is in development, with more details arriving soon. What are your thoughts on Resident Evil Requiem becoming February’s best-selling game? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Crimson Desert reaching a major sales milestone within the first day. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Resident Evil Requiem is Currently the Best-Selling Game of 2026
item.content:encoded:
New video game sales data reveal that Resident Evil Requiem is leading the charts of best-selling games of 2026.

A report from earlier this week confirmed that Resident Evil Requiem was the fastest-selling game in the series, having already sold 6 million units. This bump has helped Capcom’s latest horror survival instalment climb the video games sales charts, making it one of the top releases of 2026.

Resident Evil Requiem Leads 2026’s Best-Selling Games

Mat Piscatella of Circana has revealed the latest video game sales stats, revealing Resident Evil Requiem is currently the best-selling video game of 2026. It is followed by titles such as NBA 2K26, Call of Duty: Black Ops 7, Madden NFL 26, and Minecraft. Additional details reveal that Requiem’s launch-week dollar sales were over 60% higher than Resident Evil Village’s total, and unit sales increased by over 40%.

Additionally, the release of Resident Evil Requiem, coupled with a 27% increase in subscription services, has contributed to keeping video game content spending stable. The new title “ranked 1st in February across the PlayStation, Xbox, and the aggregated PC storefront charts.” It fell to the sixth position for Nintendo platforms, with the new titles Mario Tennis Fever and Dragon Quest 7: Reimagined taking the top spots.

In other news, Resident Evil Requiem’s director has confirmed that a story expansion is in development, with more details arriving soon. Additionally, Grace Ashcroft’s voice actor reflects on the impressive performances delivered by the voice actor for Leon Kennedy. What are your thoughts on Resident Evil Requiem becoming the best-selling game of 2026? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Starfield leading the PlayStation Store pre-order charts, with Premium Edition on top. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Starfield Tops PlayStation Pre-Order Charts
item.content:encoded:
Starfield has reached the top of the PlayStation pre-order charts following its announcement for the PS5 and PS5 Pro.

The PS5 release date for Starfield was announced earlier this week, with the developers also unveiling the Terran Armada DLC and the Free Lanes update. Both the expansion and update are set to arrive with the PS5 version of Bethesda’s space-faring adventure, and players on the new platform seem pretty hyped for it.

Starfield Premium Edition is the Most-Ordered PS5 Game Right Now

According to TheGamer, the PlayStation pre-order charts show that the $69.99 Starfield Premium Edition is currently ranked number one. Following it are two other new games that arrive later this year: the standard editions of LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight and IO Interactive’s 007 First Light. Additionally, the standard edition ($49.99) of Starfield for PS5 ranks fourth, indicating the game is dominating PlayStation’s pre-order charts a month before its release.

The standard edition of Starfield only provides players with the base game, whereas the Premium Edition comes with a boatload of content. Purchasing the pricier version will provide players with access to the paid DLCs, Terran Armada DLC, and Shattered Space, along with 1,000 Creation Credits, Constellation Skin Pack, Digital Artbook, and Original Soundtrack. Which Starfield edition did you purchase on the PS5?

In other news, Todd Howard confirmed that Bethesda games will use the AI-driven DLSS 5 technology going forward, starting with Starfield. Additionally, a new claim states that Starfield’s Switch 2 port has been delayed and may never be released. What are your thoughts on Starfield topping PlayStation pre-order charts? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Crimson Desert reaching a major sales milestone on the first day. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: Today’s Wordle #1736: Hints and Answer for March 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Today’s tips and strategies can help you solve it faster and maintain your streak—if you’re open to a little extra help. So, if you’ve found yourself stuck with today’s Wordle, don’t worry, below you’ll find some helpful hints and even the answer for Wordle Game #1736 for March 21, 2026

To play today’s Wordle, head to the game’s website. If you want to go right to today’s answer without any tips or tricks, you can click right here.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s and yesterday’s editions of Wordle. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How To Play Wordle

Wordle is a simple but addictive word puzzle. Each day, players have to guess a five-letter word answer in no more than six attempts. After each guess:

  • Green letters are correct and in the right position.
  • Yellow letters are correct, but in the wrong position.
  • Gray letters are not in the word at all.

The goal is to guess the word in as few tries as possible. Daily puzzles keep players coming back for more, making it a fun brain exercise and a popular online trend.

Wordle can only be played on the New York Times website. There is currently no app for you to download the official game.

Wordle Hints For Today, March 21, 2026

The Wordle answer for game #1736 is pretty easy, especially compared to the last few days. According to the NY Times Wordle review, today’s answer takes an average of 3.3 out of 6 guesses. That makes it nearly two guesses easier than the 5-guess average from March 20.

General Wordle Hints For March 21

Without giving today’s Wordle answer completely away, here are a few helpful hints for today to get you started:

  • Hint 1: This word is often used to describe texture or movement.
  • Hint 2: It can apply to both physical surfaces and behavior.
  • Hint 3: The tone can be either positive or negative, depending on context.
  • Hint 4: You’re more likely to hear this word in casual descriptions than formal writing.

Use these clues to narrow down your guesses and increase your chances of solving Wordle #1736 for March 21, 2026.

Starter Words For March 21, 2026

If you want help getting started without having today’s Wordle answer given to you, there are a few starter words worth trying that will help you figure out some included letters. A few starter words that may help are:

  • LICKS
  • CLICK
  • SKILL
  • SLICE

Today’s Wordle Answer For Game #1736 On March 21, 2026

If you’ve tried everything, including using the above hints, and are still stuck, you can find the answer to today’s Wordle below.

Answer: SLICK

If you solved today’s Wordle without our answer, congratulations! If not, take note of the best tips above for tomorrow’s puzzle, and check back daily for more hints.

What Does Today’s Wordle Answer Mean?

SLICK can be used as a noun, adjective, verb, or even an adverb. As a noun, it means “a smooth or slippery place or spot or the substance causing it.” As an adjective, it can mean “sly; shrewdly adroit.”

Yesterday’s Wordle Answer

If you missed the previous game, here’s yesterday’s Wordle answer for Game #1735 on March 20, 2026

Answer: OASIS

While not always the case, looking at previous words can sometimes give you insight into common patterns or letter usage in Wordle puzzles.

When Is The Next Wordle Game After #1736?

Wordle games are updated daily at 12 am or Midnight in your respective time zone. Information about the next game is often available in the hours before to give players a heads up. The game on March 22 will be Wordle Game #1737.

What Is Wordle?

Wordle is a daily word puzzle created by Josh Wardle. Initially developed as a prototype in 2013, it wasn’t fully released until 2021. The game challenges players to guess a five-letter word in six tries or fewer, using hints from each attempt to narrow down the answer. Its straightforward concept, accessibility, and the thrill of solving a new puzzle each day helped it surge in popularity by late 2021. The success of Wordle inspired numerous spin-offs and social media challenges, leading to its acquisition by The New York Times in early 2022.

For even more Insider Gaming delivered straight to your inbox, sign up for our weekly newsletter

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Nintendo Switch 2 To Get Replaceable Battery Version For EU
item.content:encoded:
In a move to align with the European Union’s policy, Nintendo will release a version of the Switch 2 that allows users to replace the battery easily.

The Switch 2 has recently received a major update introducing the Handheld Mode Boost feature, enhancing game performance on the console. Now, Nintendo is developing a new version of its flagship console to comply with the European Union’s 2023 policy on handheld consoles.

Switch 2 to Follow ‘Right to Repair’ Policy With Replaceable Battery Version

According to a new report by Nikkei, Nintendo is complying with the European Union’s “right to repair” policy to help make its console less impactful on the environment. To do this, the manufacturer will have to change the console’s specifications to provide the buyers with a way to replace the Switch 2’s battery with ease. The same version could arrive in Japan and the United States as well if there is an increase in right to repair awareness in those regions.

In 2023, the European Union announced a new regulation requiring all handheld consoles to feature replaceable batteries by 2027. This regulation would not have impacted Nintendo’s previous console; however, their current-generation console is expected to remain on the market beyond 2027 and will need to comply with this new policy. As a result, the original Switch may be discontinued in the EU by that time unless a version with replaceable batteries is released. Players should wait for an official announcement for confirmation.

In other news, THQ Nordic has announced that it’s bringing several games to the Nintendo Switch family of consoles. Additionally, Final Fantasy 7 Remake Intergrade’s director has stated that the Switch 2 edition is not a port of the PS4 version. What are your thoughts on Switch 2 getting a replaceable battery version? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the Harlem Globetrotters getting a new video game. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
